evals.prompt: Add MatrixScan Batch to my Capacitor app. Here's my entry point: EmptyApp.js. I need to track EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes and log each tracked barcode's data to the console.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, writes a complete BarcodeBatch integration into EmptyApp.js including plugin init, context creation, camera setup using BarcodeBatch.createRecommendedCameraSettings(), BarcodeBatchSettings with EAN-13 and Code 128 enabled, new BarcodeBatch(settings) + context.setMode, a didUpdateSession listener that logs tracked barcodes, DataCaptureView.forContext + connectToElement, camera on, and barcodeBatch.isEnabled = true. Shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown (mentions scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core, scandit-capacitor-datacapture-barcode, and npx cap sync)
Setup checklist mentions iOS Info.plist NSCameraUsageDescription
await ScanditCaptureCorePlugin.initializePlugins() is present and awaited before any other Scandit call
DataCaptureContext.initialize( is present
'-- ENTER YOUR SCANDIT LICENSE KEY HERE --' is present
BarcodeBatch.createRecommendedCameraSettings() is called
Camera.withSettings( is called
context.setFrameSource(camera) or context.setFrameSource(window.camera) is called
new BarcodeBatchSettings() is present
Symbology.EAN13UPCA is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
new BarcodeBatch(settings) is present (context NOT passed to BarcodeBatch constructor)
context.setMode( is called with barcodeBatch
barcodeBatch.addListener( is called with a didUpdateSession callback
session.trackedBarcodes is accessed inside the didUpdateSession callback
DataCaptureView.forContext( is present
connectToElement( is called on the view
barcodeBatch.isEnabled = true is present
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) is called
Import from 'scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core' is present
Import from 'scandit-capacitor-datacapture-barcode' is present

evals.prompt: I want to vary the highlight color for tracked barcodes based on symbology in my MatrixScan Batch Capacitor app. EAN-13 barcodes should show green, all others blue. Use BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay with brushForTrackedBarcode.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets a listener on BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay with brushForTrackedBarcode that returns a green Brush for EAN13UPCA and a blue Brush for all other symbologies. Adds a note that brushForTrackedBarcode requires the MatrixScan AR add-on. Existing integration code is preserved.

assertions.text: basicOverlay.listener = { is set with a brushForTrackedBarcode callback
brushForTrackedBarcode checks trackedBarcode.barcode.symbology === Symbology.EAN13UPCA (or equivalent)
A green-ish Brush is returned for EAN13UPCA
A blue-ish (or different) Brush is returned as default / for other symbologies
new Brush( is used to construct at least one brush
A comment or note mentions that brushForTrackedBarcode requires the MatrixScan AR add-on (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: I want to hide the highlight for barcodes starting with '0' in my MatrixScan Batch Capacitor app. All other barcodes should keep the default highlight.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets a brushForTrackedBarcode listener on BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay that returns a transparent Brush (or null) for barcodes whose data starts with '0', and returns the default brush otherwise. Mentions AR add-on requirement.

assertions.text: brushForTrackedBarcode callback is set on basicOverlay.listener
trackedBarcode.barcode.data is checked for startsWith('0') or equivalent prefix check
A transparent brush or null is returned for barcodes starting with '0'
The transparent brush has zero or near-zero alpha, OR null is returned (semantic)
A non-null brush or the overlay's default brush is returned for other barcodes
A comment or note mentions that brushForTrackedBarcode requires the MatrixScan AR add-on (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add AR bubble annotations above each tracked barcode in my Capacitor MatrixScan Batch app. Each bubble should show the barcode's data. Use BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay using new BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay(barcodeBatch), sets a listener with anchorForTrackedBarcode (Anchor.TopCenter) and offsetForTrackedBarcode (0, -1 Fraction), and inside didUpdateSession calls advancedOverlay.setViewForTrackedBarcode with a TrackedBarcodeView built from a DOM element showing the barcode data. Notes that this requires the MatrixScan AR add-on.

assertions.text: new BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay( is constructed with barcodeBatch
view.addOverlay( is called with the advanced overlay
advancedOverlay.listener is set with anchorForTrackedBarcode and offsetForTrackedBarcode callbacks
anchorForTrackedBarcode returns Anchor.TopCenter or an equivalent anchor
offsetForTrackedBarcode returns a PointWithUnit with a negative vertical offset using MeasureUnit.Fraction
TrackedBarcodeView.withHTMLElement( is called to create the serialized view
advancedOverlay.setViewForTrackedBarcode( is called with the TrackedBarcodeView and trackedBarcode
The bubble DOM element shows the barcode data (trackedBarcode.barcode.data) (semantic)
A comment or note mentions that BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay requires the MatrixScan AR add-on (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: Add lifecycle management to my MatrixScan Batch Capacitor app so that scanning pauses when the app goes to the background and resumes when it comes back to the foreground.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds Capacitor App.addListener('appStateChange', ...) (or equivalent visibility/focus listeners) that pauses BarcodeBatch and camera when the app becomes inactive, and resumes them when active. Alternatively it documents window focus/blur or Capacitor pause/resume events as the correct mechanism.

assertions.text: An app state change listener is registered (App.addListener, document.addEventListener('pause'), or window focus/blur)
barcodeBatch.isEnabled = false is called when the app goes to background / becomes inactive
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.Off) is called when the app goes to background
barcodeBatch.isEnabled = true is called when the app returns to foreground
camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.On) is called when the app returns to foreground
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: Show me how to properly clean up MatrixScan Batch in my Capacitor app when the user navigates away from the scanning screen.
evals.expected_output: The skill provides a teardown function (or augments the existing uninitialize function) that switches the camera off, sets barcodeBatch.isEnabled = false, and calls view.detachFromElement(). Explains when to call it.

assertions.text: camera.switchToDesiredState(FrameSourceState.Off) is called in the teardown function
barcodeBatch.isEnabled = false is set in the teardown function
view.detachFromElement() is called in the teardown function
The teardown function is explained as being called on navigation away or screen exit (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: I'd rather have the tracked-barcode highlights show as dots instead of rectangular frames in my MatrixScan Batch Capacitor app. Right now the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay is using the frame style. Switch it to the dot style.
evals.expected_output: The skill changes the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay style argument from BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Frame to BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Dot in the overlay constructor (the second argument of new BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay(barcodeBatch, style)). Explains that Frame and Dot are the two available styles. Existing integration code is preserved.

assertions.text: new BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay( is called with BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Dot as the style argument
BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Frame is NOT the style passed to the overlay constructor in the updated code
The two available styles are described as BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Frame and BarcodeBatchBasicOverlayStyle.Dot (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch Capacitor app I want to react when the user taps one of the highlighted tracked barcodes on the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay — log the tapped barcode's data. The basic overlay is already created.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets the basicOverlay.listener with a didTapTrackedBarcode callback that receives (overlay, trackedBarcode) and logs trackedBarcode.barcode.data. Notes that the basic-overlay listener requires the MatrixScan AR add-on. Existing integration code is preserved.

assertions.text: basicOverlay.listener is set with a didTapTrackedBarcode callback
didTapTrackedBarcode receives the overlay and trackedBarcode parameters
trackedBarcode.barcode.data is read inside the didTapTrackedBarcode callback
A comment or note mentions that the BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay listener requires the MatrixScan AR add-on (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: I have AR bubbles on tracked barcodes via BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay in my Capacitor app. I want a tap on a bubble to open a detail view — for now just log the barcode data of the tapped bubble.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a didTapViewForTrackedBarcode callback to the advancedOverlay.listener that receives (overlay, trackedBarcode) and logs trackedBarcode.barcode.data. Notes that BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay requires the MatrixScan AR add-on. Existing AR overlay code is preserved.

assertions.text: advancedOverlay.listener has a didTapViewForTrackedBarcode callback
didTapViewForTrackedBarcode receives the overlay and trackedBarcode parameters
trackedBarcode.barcode.data is read inside the didTapViewForTrackedBarcode callback
didTapTrackedBarcode is NOT used for the advanced overlay tap (that is the basic-overlay callback)
A comment or note mentions that BarcodeBatchAdvancedOverlay requires the MatrixScan AR add-on (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Batch Capacitor app I keep my own dictionary of tracked barcodes keyed by tracking identifier. I need to remove entries when a barcode leaves the frame so the dictionary doesn't grow forever. How do I detect barcodes that were lost?
evals.expected_output: The skill reads session.removedTrackedBarcodes (an array of string identifiers) inside the didUpdateSession callback and deletes the matching entries from the app's own state map. Explains that removedTrackedBarcodes contains the identifiers of barcodes lost this frame, and warns not to retain the session arrays outside the callback. Existing integration code is preserved.

assertions.text: session.removedTrackedBarcodes is accessed inside the didUpdateSession callback
session.removedTrackedBarcodes is described as an array of tracking identifiers of barcodes lost this frame (semantic)
The removed identifiers are used to delete the matching entries from the app's own state map
A note warns not to retain the session or its arrays outside the didUpdateSession callback (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)

evals.prompt: I want my MatrixScan Batch Capacitor app to play a beep and vibrate the first time each new barcode is tracked. BarcodeBatch doesn't seem to have a feedback property like BarcodeCapture does. How do I add success feedback?
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that BarcodeBatch has no built-in feedback, so feedback must be emitted manually. It constructs a Feedback (e.g. new Feedback(Vibration.defaultVibration, Sound.defaultSound), imported from scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core) once, stored outside the listener, and calls feedback.emit() inside didUpdateSession for each newly added barcode (iterating session.addedTrackedBarcodes). Existing integration code is preserved.

assertions.text: A note explains that BarcodeBatch has no built-in feedback and feedback must be emitted manually (semantic)
A Feedback instance is created, either via new Feedback( or via Feedback.defaultFeedback (semantic)
Feedback is imported from 'scandit-capacitor-datacapture-core'
feedback.emit() is called inside the didUpdateSession callback
session.addedTrackedBarcodes is iterated so feedback fires only for newly tracked barcodes (semantic)
The Feedback instance is constructed once outside the listener, not rebuilt on every frame (semantic)
Existing integration code (context, barcodeBatch, camera, view) is still referenced and used (semantic)
